hero.headline: Supabase Auth: Complete Review for Next.js Developers
hero.subheadline: A thorough breakdown of Supabase Auth's pricing, developer experience, and fit for Next.js App Router projects.

intro_paragraphs: Supabase Auth serves a specific role in the Next.js auth ecosystem. Supabase Auth is a built-in authentication service within the Supabase platform. The easy learning curve and 15 minutes setup time make it accessible for teams of all sizes.
who_should_use_paragraphs: Supabase Auth works best for Next.js projects where ease of integration matters most. The easy learning curve means your whole team can be productive within hours. We recommend it for teams that want a managed solution with minimal ops overhead.
pricing_context: Supabase Auth's free tier (50K MAU) is the most generous on this list, making it perfect for prototyping and early-stage apps. The Pro plan at $75/mo includes 100K MAU, which scales reasonably. The trap is that Supabase is not just auth - you will likely also use their database, storage, and realtime features, which all have separate usage costs. A typical production app at scale can easily cost $200-500/mo across all Supabase services. If you want auth-only without the platform dependency, Firebase Auth offers a similar pricing model without requiring you to use Firebase's database. For pure auth needs, Clerk's $199/mo Pro plan may actually be cheaper than Supabase's total platform cost at scale.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth good for Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Next.js integration score of 7/10. Supabase Auth integrates with Next. Supabase Auth is a capable option for App Router projects.

faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Supabase Auth's free tier (50K MAU) is the most generous on this list, making it perfect for prototyping and early-stage apps. Factor in your expected MAU when evaluating total cost.
